DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When data is created or modified, the data MUST have a complete and recoverable duplicate that is stored in a physically separate data center.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN08 Replicate Data to Multiple Locations
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that data is replicated across multiple physical locations to protect against data loss due to hardware failures, natural disasters, or other catastrophic events.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: Implement automated data replication processes to ensure that data is consistently duplicated in another region or availability zone. Regularly test data recovery from the replicated location to ensure integrity and availability.
STATUSEXTENDED: No Backup Vault exist.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When backups are created for disaster recovery purposes, the storage mechanism MUST NOT allow modification or deletion within 30 days of creation.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN14 Maintain Recent Backups
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that all backups used for disaster recovery are recent and subject to a retention policy that limits deletion.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: Use immutable storage solutions where possible. Implement backup retention policies that enforce a minimum retention period of 30 days.

REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN14 Maintain Recent Backups
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that all backups used for disaster recovery are recent and subject to a retention policy that limits deletion.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When a port is exposed for non-SSH network traffic, all traffic MUST include a TLS handshake AND be encrypted using TLS 1.3 or higher.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN01 Encrypt Data for Transmission
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that all communications are encrypted in transit to protect data integrity and confidentiality.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: Most cloud services enable TLS 1.3 by default. Where it is not already set, ensure that your services are configured or updated accordingly.
STATUSEXTENDED: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 viewers can use HTTP or HTTPS.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When the service receives unencrypted traffic, then it MUST either block the request or automatically redirect it to the secure equivalent.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN01 Encrypt Data for Transmission
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that all communications are encrypted in transit to protect data integrity and confidentiality.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: Review firewall, load balancer, and application configurations to ensure insecure protocols such as HTTP, FTP, and Telnet are not exposed. Where possible, implement automatic redirection to secure protocols such as HTTPS, SFTP, SSH, and regularly scan for protocol drift.
STATUSEXTENDED: CloudFront Distribution E9RGNANRAOBK0 viewers can use HTTP or HTTPS.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When the service is operational, disabling the logs for the service or its child resources MUST NOT be possible without also disabling the corresponding resource.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN09 Ensure Integrity of Access Logs
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that access logs are always recorded to an external location that cannot be manipulated from the context of the service(s) it contains logs for.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: No normal business operations should disable logs, as this could indicate an attempt to cover up unauthorized access. Ensure that logging mechanisms are tightly integrated with service operations, so that logging cannot be disabled without stopping the service itself.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When suspicious API requests are detected, the associated events MUST be logged, including the source details, time, and nature of the activity.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: Controls that collect, alert, and retain IAM-related events.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.IAM.CN10 Alert On Anomalous Behaviour
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that logs and associated alerts are generated when anomalous API requests are made by a single identity, such as API requests commonly associated with privilege escalation tactics, originating from an external or malicious IP address or performed by a previously dormant identity, which may indicate that credentals may be compromised, as well as for password brute-force attempts and account lockouts.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When the service is operational, disabling the logs for the service or its child resources MUST NOT be possible without also disabling the corresponding resource.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN09 Ensure Integrity of Access Logs
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that access logs are always recorded to an external location that cannot be manipulated from the context of the service(s) it contains logs for.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: No normal business operations should disable logs, as this could indicate an attempt to cover up unauthorized access. Ensure that logging mechanisms are tightly integrated with service operations, so that logging cannot be disabled without stopping the service itself.
STATUSEXTENDED: No CloudTrail trails enabled with logging were found.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When the service is operational, any attempt to redirect logs for the service or its child resources MUST NOT be possible without halting operation of the corresponding resource and publishing corresponding events to monitored channels.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN09 Ensure Integrity of Access Logs
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that access logs are always recorded to an external location that cannot be manipulated from the context of the service(s) it contains logs for.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: No normal business operations should result in the redirection of logs, as this could indicate an attempt to cover up unauthorized access. Ensure that logging configurations are immutable during service operation so that any changes require stopping the service and publishing corresponding events to monitored channels.

REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Logging & Monitoring control family ensures that access, changes, and security-relevant events are captured, monitored, and alerted on in order to provide visibility, support incident response, and meet compliance requirements.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN04 Log All Access and Changes
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that all access attempts are logged to maintain a detailed audit trail for security and compliance purposes.
STATUSEXTENDED: No CloudWatch log groups found with metric filters or alarms associated.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When the service is operational, any attempt to redirect logs for the service or its child resources MUST NOT be possible without halting operation of the corresponding resource and publishing corresponding events to monitored channels.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN09 Ensure Integrity of Access Logs
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that access logs are always recorded to an external location that cannot be manipulated from the context of the service(s) it contains logs for.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: No normal business operations should result in the redirection of logs, as this could indicate an attempt to cover up unauthorized access. Ensure that logging configurations are immutable during service operation so that any changes require stopping the service and publishing corresponding events to monitored channels.
STATUSEXTENDED: No CloudWatch log groups found with metric filters or alarms associated.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When data is created or modified, the data MUST have a complete and recoverable duplicate that is stored in a physically separate data center.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN08 Replicate Data to Multiple Locations
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that data is replicated across multiple physical locations to protect against data loss due to hardware failures, natural disasters, or other catastrophic events.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: Implement automated data replication processes to ensure that data is consistently duplicated in another region or availability zone. Regularly test data recovery from the replicated location to ensure integrity and availability.
STATUSEXTENDED: DynamoDB table Testtable is not protected by a backup plan.

DESCRIPTION: Common Cloud Controls Catalog (CCC) for AWS
REQUIREMENTS_DESCRIPTION: When backups are created for disaster recovery purposes, the most recent backup MUST have a creation date within the past 30 days.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_FAMILYDESCRIPTIO: The Data control family ensures the confidentiality, integrity, availability, and sovereignty of data across its lifecycle. These controls govern how data is transmitted, stored, replicated, and protected from unauthorized access, tampering, or exposure beyond defined trust perimeters.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SECTION: CCC.Core.CN14 Maintain Recent Backups
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Ensure that all backups used for disaster recovery are recent and subject to a retention policy that limits deletion.
REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_RECOMMENDATION: Implement automated backup processes to ensure that backups are created regularly. Monitor backup schedules and verify that the most recent backup creation date is within the last 30 days.

REQUIREMENTS_ATTRIBUTES_SUBSECTIONOBJECT: Establish a formal program for quality evaluation and adversarial testing (red teaming) to ensure GenAI system meet all business, quality, security and compliance requirements before getting deployed into production environments.
